title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jaw clenching
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jaw clenching are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are taken orally to alleviate the symptoms of teeth grinding and jaw clenching. These tinctures often include valerian root, passionflower, and skullcap, all known for their calming and relaxing properties. By reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, these herbal remedies can improve sleep quality, reduce headaches and facial pain, and enhance overall well-being. One satisfied user shared that after incorporating herbal tinctures into their nighttime routine, they experienced fewer instances of jaw clenching, leading to more restful sleep and improved daily functioning.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet flag tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains properties that act as a muscle relaxant and anti-spasmodic. These properties help to alleviate tension in the jaw muscles, reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching. Additionally, sweet flag tincture has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce any swelling or pain in the jaw area, providing further relief. Overall, sweet flag tincture offers a natural and effective solution for those struggling with jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Sweet flag tincture helps relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, alleviating jaw clenching due to its active constituents, such as asarone and eugenol.
Analgesic: It provides pain relief for tension and discomfort in the jaw area, thanks to its active compounds like methyl eugenol and sesquiterpene.
Anxiolytic: Sweet flag tincture can help manage stress and anxiety, which often contribute to jaw clenching, by modulating GABA receptors and inhibiting acetylcholinesterase.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to compounds like β-asarone and eugenol, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw muscles.

Neuroprotective: The neuroprotective effects of sweet flag tincture, resulting from its antioxidant and anti-apoptotic properties, can protect and support nerve health, reducing the risk of chronic jaw clenching and related neuropathic pain.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of sweet flag plant contain sedative properties that help to relax the muscles, making it effective in treating jaw clenching.
Leaves: The leaves of the sweet flag plant have antispasmodic properties that help to alleviate muscle spasms, including those in the jaw.
Flowers: The flowers of the sweet flag plant contain calming properties that help to reduce anxiety and tension, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
Seeds: The seeds of the sweet flag plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw.
Buds: The buds of the sweet flag plant contain high concentrations of volatile oils that have a soothing effect on the nervous system, making it useful in treating jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest acorus calamus rhizomes selecting fresh and clean material with no signs of mold or mildew.
Dry the harvested rhizomes for at least 7 days to reduce moisture levels allowing easier grinding.
Grind dried acorus calamus rhizomes into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Combine 1 part ground rhizome with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids after filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cohosh tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains phytoestrogens, which are plant-based compounds that can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. Estrogen plays a crucial role in regulating muscle tension, and fluctuations in estrogen levels can lead to muscle tightness and discomfort. By helping to balance estrogen levels, black cohosh tincture can reduce muscle tension in the jaw, alleviating symptoms of jaw clenching and promoting a greater sense of relaxation. Additionally, black cohosh has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can further help to reduce muscle pain and inflammation in the jaw. Overall, black cohosh tincture offers a natural and effective way to manage jaw clenching and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Black cohosh tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw, making it easier to manage clenching. The active constituents responsible for this property are triterpene glycosides and isoflavones.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of black cohosh tincture help alleviate pain associated with jaw clenching. This is due to the presence of compounds like actein and cimicifugin.

Muscle relaxant: Black cohosh tincture has muscle relaxant properties that can help reduce tension in the jaw muscles, thus preventing clenching. This is attributed to the presence of isoflavones and alkaloids.
Anxiolytic: Black cohosh tincture can help reduce anxiety, which can often lead to jaw clenching. This is due to the presence of isoflavones, which have been shown to have anxiolytic effects.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of black cohosh tincture can help relieve muscle spasms and cramps in the jaw, providing relief from clenching. This is attributed to the presence of triterpene glycosides and isoflavones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of black cohosh contain active compounds that have been shown to help relax muscles, making them ideal for treating jaw clenching.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of black cohosh contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
Flowers: The flowers of black cohosh contain flavonoids that have been shown to have muscle relaxant properties, making them useful for treating jaw clenching.
Seeds: The seeds of black cohosh contain essential oils that have been used in traditional medicine to treat muscle spasms, including those associated with jaw clenching.
Bark: The bark of black cohosh contains triterpene glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-spasmodic effects, making it useful for treating jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried or fresh black cohosh root material from reputable sources weighing approximately one pound.
Cleanly chop the dried black cohosh root into small pieces and grind them into a coarse powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground black cohosh powder with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of one part herb to three parts liquid in glass jars.
Store the jarred mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking the jars daily allowing the tincture to steep and extract.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or coffee filters into another clean container discarding the solids after pressing out as much liquid as possible.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains a variety of compounds, including anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing properties. These properties can help to reduce muscle tension and spasms in the jaw, which are often the cause of jaw clenching. Additionally, oats tincture may also help to soothe and calm the nervous system, which can help to alleviate the stress and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching. The herbal extracts found in oats tincture, such as Avena sativa, have been used traditionally to treat nerve-related conditions and promote relaxation. Overall, oats tincture can provide a natural and effective way to manage jaw clenching and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Oats tincture contains avenanthramides, which have been shown to have muscle relaxant properties, helping to alleviate jaw clenching.
Anti-inflammatory: Oats tincture includes antioxidants like avenanthramides and tocols, which reduce inflammation, helping to minimize tension and discomfort in the jaw.
Nervine: Oats tincture has nervine properties due to the presence of nervine alkaloids, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, addressing one of the potential causes of jaw clenching.
Analgesic: Oats tincture contains compounds like salicylic acid and avenacosides, which have analgesic properties, helping to alleviate pain associated with jaw clenching.

Adaptogenic: Oats tincture has adaptogenic properties due to its high content of beta-glucans, allowing the body to better cope with stress and regulate cortisol levels, potentially reducing jaw clenching caused by stress or anxiety.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Oat seeds are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for jaw clenching due to their high concentration of nervine compounds that help relax the muscles and reduce tension.
Leaves: Oat leaves contain soothing properties that can help alleviate muscle spasms, making them useful for creating tinctures to treat jaw clenching.
Flowers: Oat flowers have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties, making them ideal for reducing inflammation and stress-related jaw clenching.
Stems: Oat stems have astringent properties that can help tone and relax muscles, making them useful for creating tinctures to treat jaw clenching.
Roots: Oat roots have a high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing properties, making them ideal for treating jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa seeds and clean them thoroughly to remove debris and impurities.
Grind the cleaned seeds into a coarse powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine one part of the powdered oat seeds with three parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily for eight hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and label.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains alkaloids that act as natural muscle relaxants. These alkaloids help to reduce muscle tension and spasms, which can alleviate the symptoms of jaw clenching. Yanhusuo has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including anxiety and pain. Its active compounds, such as tetrahydropalmatine, work by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, which can help to reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation. By reducing muscle tension in the jaw, yanhusuo tincture can help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with jaw clenching, providing relief for those who suffer from this condition.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Yanhusuo tincture helps relax muscles, reducing jaw clenching due to its active constituent, Ligustilide, which has been shown to have muscle relaxant properties.
Anti-anxiety: Yanhusuo tincture has anti-anxiety effects that can alleviate jaw clenching caused by stress or anxiety, thanks to its active compound, Tetramethylpyrazine.
Analgesic: Yanhusuo tincture contains pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce discomfort from jaw clenching, attributed to its active constituents, Ferulic acid and Senkyunolide I.

Anti-inflammatory: Yanhusuo tincture's anti-inflammatory effects can help decrease swelling and inflammation caused by excessive jaw clenching, owing to its active compounds, Ferulic acid and Senkyunolide I.

Neuroprotective: Yanhusuo tincture protects nerves and promotes nerve health, which can help prevent or reduce jaw clenching caused by nerve damage or dysfunction, due to its active constituent, Tetramethylpyrazine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yanhusuo's roots are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for jaw clenching due to their high concentration of alkaloids that help relax muscles.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of yanhusuo contain active compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them useful for reducing jaw clenching.
Flowers: Although less commonly used, yanhusuo's flowers can be included in tinctures for jaw clenching, as they contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate muscle tension.
Seeds: Yanhusuo seeds are rich in essential oils and fatty acids, which can help soothe and relax the muscles, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried corydalis yanhusuo roots and stems in clean, dry containers.
Chop the dried herb into small pieces using a sharp knife or food processor for 10 minutes.
Combine the chopped herb with 250ml of 95% ethanol and 50ml of distilled water in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to extract the active compounds at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: California poppy tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains natural compounds that have a calming and relaxing effect on the body. The tincture can help reduce muscle tension and anxiety, which are often contributing factors to jaw clenching and teeth grinding. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, California poppy tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of jaw clenching and improve overall oral health. Additionally, the herb has been shown to have pain-relieving properties, which can further help to reduce discomfort associated with jaw clenching. Overall, California poppy tincture offers a natural and holistic approach to managing jaw clenching and promoting relaxation.
remedy_properties: Analgesic property: California poppy tincture can help alleviate jaw clenching pain by containing alkaloids such as protopine and allocryptopine, which interact with opioid receptors to reduce pain perception.

Anxiolytic property: The tincture can help manage jaw clenching caused by anxiety, as it contains isoquinoline alkaloids that influence neurotransmitter activity, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels.

Sedative property: California poppy tincture can help reduce jaw clenching by promoting sleep and reducing muscle tension, due to the presence of alkaloids like sanguinarine and chelerythrine, which have a calming effect on the central nervous system.

Muscle relaxant property: The tincture can help alleviate jaw clenching by promoting muscle relaxation, thanks to its alkaloid content, including protopine and cryptopine, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension.

Antispasmodic property: California poppy tincture can help manage jaw clenching caused by muscle spasms, as it contains isoquinoline alkaloids that can help alleviate smooth and skeletal muscle spasms, reducing involuntary jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of alkaloids, which can help relax muscles and reduce tension, making them effective for jaw clenching.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves can help reduce inflammation and provide relief for muscle spasms and tension related to jaw clenching.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and active compounds, the seeds can help soothe and nourish the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Roots: Contain powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making the roots useful for reducing pain and inflammation associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eschscholzia californica flowers in abundance for optimal extraction quality.
Dry the harvested flowers using shade drying or dehydrator at low temperature to preserve potency.
Combine one part of dried poppy flowers with five parts of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing the botanicals to infuse and extract.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another container discarding solids after two weeks of gentle agitation daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains natural compounds that have muscle relaxant properties. These compounds, including humulones and lupulones, work by reducing muscle tension and spasms, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with jaw clenching. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of humulus lupulus may help reduce any swelling or inflammation in the jaw muscles, further contributing to its effectiveness in treating jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension in the jaw muscles, humulus lupulus tincture can provide relief from symptoms of jaw clenching and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Humulus lupulus tincture helps relax muscles, reducing tension and alleviating jaw clenching caused by muscle spasms or stiffness, due to its active constituents, such as bitter acids and essential oils.

Anxiolytic: The tincture has calming and anxiety-reducing effects, which can help prevent jaw clenching associated with stress or anxiety, thanks to its sedative properties from the humulones and lupulones.
Analgesic: Humulus lupulus tincture can alleviate pain, including the discomfort caused by persistent jaw clenching, as it contains pain-relieving compounds like beta-acids and essential oils.

Anti-inflammatory: The tincture can reduce inflammation, which may help minimize swelling and tenderness in the jaw area due to clenching, due to its active constituents, such as flavonoids and prenylflavonoids.

Antispasmodic: Humulus lupulus tincture can help relieve muscle spasms and involuntary contractions, which can reduce the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching, thanks to its active constituents, including essential oils and bitter acids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of humulus lupulus, also known as hops, contain volatile oils and bitter acids that have muscle relaxant properties, making them effective in reducing jaw clenching.
Buds: The buds of humulus lupulus are rich in essential oils and resins that have calming and relaxing effects, helping to alleviate tension in the jaw muscles.
Leaves: The leaves of humulus lupulus contain tannins and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, providing relief from jaw clenching and pain.

Stems: The stems of humulus lupulus contain beneficial compounds such as essential oils, phenols, and alkaloids that have sedative and muscle relaxant effects, making them useful for reducing jaw clenching.
Roots: The roots of humulus lupulus contain triterpenes and steroids that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate tension and pain in the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality humulus lupulus leaves and stems from trusted sources.
Cleanly sort and chop the harvested plant material into smaller pieces.
Combine one part chopped plant material with one part pure vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place for 6 weeks, shaking daily for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids after filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains active compounds, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to possess anxiety-reducing and muscle-relaxing properties. These constituents work together to help alleviate tension in the jaw muscles, reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, English lavender tincture can provide relief for individuals experiencing symptoms associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and bruxism. Regular use of this herbal remedy may contribute to improved jaw function and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: English lavender tincture can help reduce anxiety, which is important for jaw clenching as it can be a symptom of anxiety or stress. The active constituents that give it this property include linalool and linalyl acetate.

Analgesic property: It can alleviate pain, which is crucial for jaw clenching as it can cause discomfort and pain in the jaw and surrounding muscles. The active constituents responsible for this property include caryophyllene and terpinen-4-ol.

Antispasmodic property: English lavender tincture can help relax muscles, which is important for jaw clenching as it often involves tension in the jaw muscles. The active constituents that contribute to this property include linalool and lavandulol.

Sedative property: It can promote sleep and reduce restlessness, which is significant for jaw clenching as it can be exacerbated by lack of sleep or restlessness. The active constituents that give it this property include linalyl acetate and lavandulol.

Nervine property: English lavender tincture can help calm the nervous system, which is essential for jaw clenching as it can be a response to stress or anxiety. The active constituents that provide this property include linalool and linalyl acetate.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in aromatic compounds, lavender flowers are used in tinctures to help relax muscles and alleviate jaw clenching.
Leaves: Containing calming agents, lavender leaves are used in tinctures to soothe tension and reduce jaw clenching.
Buds: High in essential oils, lavender buds are used in tinctures to promote relaxation and ease jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried english lavender flowers at dawn when the fragrance is most potent and aromatic.
Combine one part dried english lavender flowers with four parts high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow the herbal extract to infuse into the solvent.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Transfer the clear tincture to dark amber glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains active compounds that have a relaxing effect on the muscles and nervous system. This herbal remedy is known for its ability to alleviate tension and anxiety, which can be contributing factors to jaw clenching or bruxism. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, motherwort tincture can help ease the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching, providing relief to individuals suffering from this condition. Its efficacy is attributed to its calming properties, making it a popular natural alternative for managing jaw clenching and its associated symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Motherwort tincture helps reduce anxiety, which can be a contributing factor to jaw clenching. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides.

Sedative: Motherwort tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate tension and reduce jaw clenching. This is due to the presence of alkaloids, such as stachydrine and leonurine.

Antispasmodic: Motherwort tincture can help relieve muscle spasms, including those in the jaw, by relaxing the muscles. This property is attributed to the presence of alkaloids and iridoid glycosides.

Hypotensive: Motherwort tincture can help lower blood pressure, which can reduce tension and stress in the body, thereby alleviating jaw clenching. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids, tannins, and flavonoids.

Nervine: Motherwort tincture has a tonic effect on the nervous system, helping to soothe and calm the mind. This can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to jaw clenching. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and are used to make medicinal tinctures for jaw clenching due to their relaxing and calming effects.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, and are used to make tinctures for their mild sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaw clenching.
Stems: Contain active compounds that have a soothing effect on the nervous system, making them suitable for medicinal tinctures used to treat jaw clenching.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils and alkaloids, and are used to make tinctures that help relax muscles, reducing the occurrence of jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried motherwort leaves and stems according to botanical quality and quantity requirements.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent interaction.
Mix one part chopped motherwort with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, sealing it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and storing the liquid in airtight glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian tobacco tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains natural muscle relaxants that can help alleviate tension in the jaw. The herbal ingredients, such as lobelia inflata and passionflower, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety, muscle spasms, and nervous restlessness. These properties can help reduce the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching, particularly in individuals who experience this symptom as a result of stress or anxiety. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, Indian tobacco tincture can provide relief for those suffering from jaw clenching and related symptoms, such as teeth grinding and headaches.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Indian tobacco tincture helps relax muscles, which is important for jaw clenching as it can alleviate tension and discomfort in the jaw caused by constant clenching. The active constituents responsible for this property are the alkaloids found in the plant, such as lobeline.

Analgesic: It can help manage pain, which is crucial for jaw clenching as it can reduce the discomfort and soreness that results from constant muscle contraction. The presence of analgesic compounds like lobeline contributes to this property.

Sedative: Indian tobacco tincture has a calming effect, which is significant for jaw clenching because it can help decrease anxiety and stress levels, reducing the likelihood of clenching and grinding teeth. The alkaloids, including lobeline and nicotine, play a role in inducing this sedative effect.

Antispasmodic: It can help alleviate muscle spasms, which is important for jaw clenching since it can reduce the frequency and intensity of involuntary muscle contractions in the jaw. The antispasmodic properties can be attributed to the alkaloids and other active constituents in the plant.

Neuroprotective: Indian tobacco tincture has neuroprotective effects, which are relevant for jaw clenching as they can help safeguard nerve cells from damage caused by constant muscle contraction. The presence of antioxidants and other beneficial compounds contributes to this neuroprotective property.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that help relax muscles, making them useful for jaw clenching tinctures.
Leaves: Rich in soothing properties, leaves are often used in tinctures to alleviate tension in the jaw.
Flowers: Packed with calming agents, flowers can be used in tinctures to help reduce jaw clenching caused by anxiety or stress.
Seeds: High in medicinal benefits, seeds can be used in tinctures to help manage muscle spasms and relax the jaw.
Bark: Contains compounds that help relieve pain and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice for jaw clenching tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Gather one pound of fresh lobelia inflata leaves and flowers for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested botanicals into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped lobelia with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part herbs to three parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to enhance solubilization.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean amber bottle and store it in a dark cupboard for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with jaw clenching because it contains active compounds, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, that have anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties. These components can help alleviate tension in the jaw muscles, reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching. Additionally, German chamomile has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, German chamomile tincture can provide relief for individuals experiencing jaw clenching and associated discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: German chamomile tincture can help relax the muscles and reduce jaw clenching due to its antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol.
Anxiolytic: By alleviating anxiety, this tincture may help diminish tension-related jaw clenching, thanks to its anxiolytic effects that are partly owed to apigenin, a flavonoid present in chamomile.
Analgesic: German chamomile tincture can help alleviate pain associated with jaw clenching, as it contains analgesic compounds, such as matricin and its derivatives, that inhibit pain transmission.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to active constituents like chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol, help reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles, providing relief from clenching.

Sedative: German chamomile tincture can promote better sleep and reduce nighttime jaw clenching, owing to its sedative effects, which are partly due to the presence of apigenin and other volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: German chamomile flowers are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, making them ideal for jaw clenching tinctures.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain high levels of essential oils, which can help alleviate muscle spasms and reduce tension in the jaw.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a great source of antioxidants and have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them useful for treating jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried german chamomile flowers ensuring approximately one cup of material for the desired yield.
Combine the harvested chamomile with high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling it almost to the top and leaving minimal headspace.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking the mixture once or twice daily allowing for extraction and steeping.
After the specified time strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.

Transfer the resulting herbal german chamomile tincture to dark glass bottles sealing them tightly and storing them in a cool dark place for up to five years with regular checking on quality and potency.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw clenching with herbal tinctures is by using natural relaxants like chamomile or valerian root. These herbs have calming properties that can help alleviate tension in the jaw muscles. Passionflower and skullcap are also effective options that can reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, thus minimizing jaw clenching. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness. Consistency in using these tinctures is key to managing jaw clenching effectively.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw clenching that are treated with herbal tinctures include teeth grinding and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. These conditions can cause discomfort, pain, and even damage to the teeth and jaw. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from valerian root, passionflower, and skullcap, can help alleviate these symptoms. Valerian root has natural muscle relaxant properties that can help reduce tension in the jaw, while passionflower and skullcap have calming effects that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. These herbal tinctures can be taken orally, usually in drop or spray form, and can be found in health food stores or online. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
